---
description: "Output timestamp discipline for phase transitions and long-running operations"
applyTo: '**'
---

# Output Timestamps

Emit a timestamp at meaningful work-output boundaries so operators can see
elapsed time between agent outputs.

## When to Stamp

Stamp at phase transitions and long-running operation boundaries, including:

* planning started / plan ready / plan blocked
* build loop started / task claimed / task completed / task blocked
* review started / review complete / findings require intervention
* runtime verification started / passed / follow-up needed / failed
* operational closure ready / ready with conditions / blocked
* long-running builds, test suites, CI polling, or multi-file generation

Do not stamp every trivial line, routine tool narration, or minor intermediate
update. The stamp is for operator-visible progress, not log spam.

## Format

Use ISO-8601 UTC followed by the elapsed time since the previous stamp in the
same session:

```text
2026-07-02T18:49:41Z (+2m13s)
```

Use compact human-readable deltas such as `+45s`, `+2m13s`, or `+1h04m`.

## First Stamp

The first stamp in a session has no previous stamp. Either omit the
parenthetical or use `(+0s)`:

```text
2026-07-02T18:47:28Z
2026-07-02T18:47:28Z (+0s)
```

## Consistency

When another instruction or capability pack requests stamped progress output,
this instruction is the format authority.
